items.title: DifyTap: Four Flaws Let Attackers Read AI Chats Across Cloud Tenants
items.desc: Zafran Security found four bugs in popular open-source agentic platform Dify that enable cross-tenant chat exfiltration.
items.summary: Zafran Security disclosed DifyTap, four vulnerabilities in Dify (146K+ GitHub stars). CVE-2026-41947, CVE-2026-41948, CVE-2026-41949 and CVE-2026-41950 allow auth bypass, path traversal to internal Plugin Daemon APIs, and reading files across tenants. Two are critical and require no authentication. Fixes except CVE-2026-41948 shipped in Dify 1.14.2.
items.importance: Multi-tenant AI platforms are becoming the default backend for agent apps. DifyTap proves that AI app infrastructure has the same tenant-isolation risks as SaaS, with the added twist that chat traces and uploaded documents are crown jewels.
items.noteworthy: Upgrade to Dify 1.14.2+ and watch for the remaining fix. Audit tenant boundaries in any AI workflow platform. Treat uploaded documents and chat traces as cross-tenant isolation failures waiting to happen.

items.title: Squidbleed: 29-Year-Old Proxy Bug Leaks HTTP Requests Between Users
items.desc: A heap over-read in Squid FTP parsing can return another user's cleartext HTTP request, including auth tokens.
items.summary: Calif.io disclosed Squidbleed (CVE-2026-47729), a heap over-read in Squid's 1997-era FTP directory parser. An attacker who shares the same Squid proxy can leak cleartext HTTP requests from other users because Squid reuses 4KB buffers without zeroing them. SUSE rates it CVSS 6.5; the vector requires proxy access and a controlled FTP server on port 21.
items.importance: Shared proxies remain common in schools, offices, and public Wi-Fi. The attack turns a trusted insider (or any same-network user) into a passive HTTP traffic sniffer for cleartext sessions.
items.noteworthy: Disable FTP in Squid unless absolutely needed; it removes the attack surface entirely. Verify the null-terminator fix in FtpGateway.cc, regardless of version number. Patch to Squid 7.6/7.7 when available.

items.title: ShapedPlugin Pro Plugins Backdoored via Official Update Channel
items.desc: Wordfence confirms a supply-chain attack pushed backdoored Pro plugin builds through ShapedPlugin's licensed EDD channel.
items.summary: Attackers compromised ShapedPlugin's build/distribution pipeline and injected backdoors into Pro plugin updates for Product Slider Pro, Real Testimonials Pro, and Smart Post Show Pro. CVE-2026-10735 (CVSS 9.8) covers the incident; Product Slider Pro alone is CVE-2026-49777 (CVSS 10.0). The malware fetches a payload from 194.76.217[.]28:2871, steals wp-config.php, admin accounts, WooCommerce orders, and SMTP credentials, then hides itself.
items.importance: This is the nightmare scenario for WordPress site owners: a legitimate vendor's official update channel ships malware. The free WordPress.org versions are unaffected, but paid-license customers got the backdoor.
items.noteworthy: If you run ShapedPlugin Pro products, reset all passwords, revoke/regenerate 2FA secrets, review admin accounts, and check mail plugin SMTP configs. Treat any third-party plugin's paid update channel as a supply-chain risk.

items.title: Legacy Infrastructure Hijacks AI Agents, Not the AI Itself
items.desc: THN analysis: attackers bypass AI security controls by exploiting unpatched servers, AD misconfig, and over-privileged IAM.
items.summary: A Gartner Summit talk highlighted that 71% of organizations are piloting AI agents and 31% have them in production, while 70% give AI systems more privileged access than humans in the same role. Attackers can chain legacy exposures (e.g., CVE-2025-24813 Tomcat, RBCD misconfig) to harvest AWS keys from a developer and take over the AI agent's cloud access without ever attacking the model.
items.importance: AI agents inherit and often amplify existing security debt. Organizations with over-privileged AI report a 76% incident rate versus 17% for least-privilege deployments. The attack surface is underneath the AI layer.
items.noteworthy: Apply least privilege to AI service accounts and IAM roles. Patch perimeter servers before deploying agents. Audit Active Directory for RBCD and constrained delegation misconfigs. Do not let AI agents outrank human users in permissions.

items.title: AryStinger Turns 4,300 Legacy Routers Into Reconnaissance Proxies
items.desc: QiAnxin XLab tracks AryStinger, a malware family infecting Realtek RTL819X routers via CVEs from 2013 and 2016.
items.summary: XLab discovered AryStinger in March 2026 spreading from 107.150.106.14. It exploits CVE-2013-3307 (Linksys) and CVE-2016-5681 (D-Link) on Realtek RTL819X hardware. About 75% of infected routers are D-Link DIR-850L, mostly in South Korea (48%) and China (32%). A second strain targets QNAP NAS via CVE-2025-11837. Infected nodes scan, fingerprint, tunnel traffic, and execute attacker-supplied Go/Java/Python code.
items.importance: Old routers and NAS boxes keep becoming state-actor infrastructure. AryStinger is not a DDoS botnet; it is a quiet footprinting and proxy network used before the real intrusion.
items.noteworthy: Retire end-of-life routers. Turn off remote admin. Monitor /tmp/bin, processes named syswapd0h/syswapd0w, and outbound connections to ajb8.com domains. Patch QNAP Malware Remover against CVE-2025-11837.

items.title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak: GPT-5.5-Cyber, Codex Security, Patch the Planet
items.desc: OpenAI launches full GPT-5.5-Cyber, a Codex Security plugin, and Patch the Planet to fix open-source bugs at scale.
items.summary: On June 22, OpenAI expanded its Daybreak program: the full version of GPT-5.5-Cyber for trusted defenders, a Codex Security plugin to find and patch vulnerabilities in developer workflows, and Patch the Planet — a partnership with Trail of Bits, HackerOne, and Calif to assist open-source maintainers. The effort is framed as OpenAI's response to Anthropic's Mythos and growing AI hacking concerns.
items.importance: AI vendors are racing to own the defensive-remediation loop, not just the model. If these tools work, they shift the economics of vulnerability discovery and patching toward machine speed.
items.noteworthy: Watch for GPT-5.5-Cyber availability if you are a vetted defender. Evaluate Codex Security for internal code review. Expect more AI-vs-AI patching stories as OpenAI and Anthropic compete on cyber capabilities.

items.title: Meta Pauses Internal AI Training Program After Companywide Data Leak
items.desc: Meta's Model Capability Initiative recorded employee keystrokes and mouse movements; the data became visible across the company.
items.summary: Business Insider reported Meta is pausing its Model Capability Initiative (MCI) after screenshots showed sensitive employee data — including private conversations, performance data, and transcriptions — was accessible companywide. The program, announced in April 2026, was mandatory for most staff and intended to improve AI models using keystroke and mouse data. Meta classified the incident as SEV 2 and is investigating.
items.importance: This is a cautionary tale for AI training data collection inside companies. Even Meta could not keep behavioral surveillance data contained, sparking internal backlash and exposing performance and chat data.
items.noteworthy: Audit any internal AI training programs that collect employee behavior. Apply strict need-to-know access and encryption. Mandate opt-out for invasive telemetry if you cannot guarantee isolation.

items.title: VibeThinker-3B Matches Flagship Reasoning Models at 3B Parameters
items.desc: WeiboAI's MIT-licensed 3B dense model scores 94.3% on AIME26 and 80.2% on LiveCodeBench v6.
items.summary: VibeThinker-3B, a 3B-parameter dense model from WeiboAI built on Qwen2.5-Coder-3B, uses curriculum SFT, multi-domain RL, and offline self-distillation. It reaches 94.3% on AIME26 (97.1% with test-time scaling), 80.2% Pass@1 on LiveCodeBench v6, and 96.1% on recent LeetCode contests, placing it alongside DeepSeek V3.2, GLM-5, and Gemini 3 Pro on verifiable reasoning tasks.
items.importance: The paper argues verifiable reasoning can be compressed into compact reasoning cores, while broad knowledge still needs large coverage. This is another data point that small, specialized models can outperform giants on narrow, verifiable tasks.
items.noteworthy: Watch for VibeThinker-3B on HuggingFace and Ollama. It is MIT-licensed and small enough to run locally. Test it against your coding and math workloads before defaulting to a 70B+ model.

items.title: Moebius: 0.22B Image Inpainting Model Hits 10B-Level Quality
items.desc: HUST VL's Moebius runs at 26ms/step and matches FLUX.1-Fill-Dev quality using under 2% of its parameters.
items.summary: Moebius is a 0.22B-parameter lightweight inpainting framework from HUST VL. It uses a Local-λ Mix Interaction block and adaptive multi-granularity distillation from PixelHacker. The model is 15x faster than 10B-level competitors, runs at 26ms/step on a single GPU, and matches or beats FLUX.1-Fill-Dev and SD3.5 Large-Inpainting across six benchmarks.
items.importance: Image inpainting is moving from foundation-model bloat to task-specific specialists. For consumer GPUs and edge devices, Moebius makes high-quality inpainting practical without a 10B model.
items.noteworthy: Check the code on GitHub (hustvl/Moebius) and the arXiv paper. Try it for object removal and image repair on your local GPU. This is the kind of efficiency trend that matters more than raw parameter counts.

items.title: AMD Ryzen AI Halo Preorders Start at ,999 — DGX Spark Rival
items.desc: AMD's compact agent computer with Ryzen AI Max+ 395 undercuts NVIDIA's DGX Spark by 00.
items.summary: AMD unveiled Ryzen AI Halo, a compact developer platform powered by the Ryzen AI Max+ 395 processor, exclusively at Micro Center with preorders starting in June 2026. The base config starts at ,999, 00 less than NVIDIA's DGX Spark. AMD claims wider OS support, leadership LLM token value, and a 50 TOPS NPU. Wider availability is planned for Q3 2026.
items.importance: Local AI compute is getting competitive. After NVIDIA's DGX Spark set the price floor, AMD is now challenging with a cheaper, NPU-equipped alternative. For local agents and small clusters, this matters.
items.noteworthy: Compare Ryzen AI Halo against your DGX Spark for price/perf and OS support. If the benchmark claims hold, it could be the better value local inference node. Watch for independent reviews once units ship.

items.title: Memory Crisis Pushes Buyers to DDR2 and DDR3, Retro RAM Prices Surge
items.desc: TrendForce reports DRAM shortages are so severe that hardware makers are downgrading designs to legacy memory.
items.summary: AI demand is consuming HBM and server DRAM capacity, leaving shortages of DDR4 and DDR5. TrendForce says some hardware vendors are redesigning products to use DDR3 or even DDR2 to secure supply. DDR2 contract prices are expected to rise 55-60% in Q2 2026 and another 35-40% in Q3. Winbond is winding down DDR2 while ESMT is maximizing production at PSMC.
items.importance: The memory shortage is no longer just about high-end AI chips. It is rippling down to legacy DRAM and affecting PC, embedded, and industrial device costs. Your next homelab or mini-PC build may cost more.
items.noteworthy: Plan memory purchases early. Consider used DDR4/DDR5 for homelab builds while prices are volatile. The shortage may persist until SK hynix and Micron bring new capacity online in 2027-2028.

items.title: US Questions ASML Over Fears an EUV Machine Reached China
items.desc: Trump administration officials told ASML executives they are concerned a top EUV lithography tool may have ended up in China.
items.summary: Reuters and Bloomberg reported on June 19 that US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick raised concerns with ASML senior leaders that one of its extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography machines may have reached China, violating export restrictions. ASML publicly denied ever shipping an EUV machine or EUV-specific components to China. EUV tools are the only systems capable of producing the most advanced processor patterns.
items.importance: This is a major escalation in semiconductor export-control enforcement. If true, it would imply a leak in the supply chain for the most sensitive chipmaking equipment. Even the accusation raises tensions in the China-US-tech triangle.
items.noteworthy: Watch for follow-up reports on whether evidence surfaces. The case reinforces that semiconductor tooling is treated as strategic infrastructure. Expect tighter controls and more audits of advanced equipment.

items.title: AWS Lambda MicroVMs Bring VM Isolation to Serverless User and AI Code
items.desc: AWS launches Lambda MicroVMs in five regions, offering Firecracker-based VM isolation with near-instant resume and up to 8-hour state.
items.summary: AWS announced Lambda MicroVMs on June 22, 2026. Built on Firecracker, the new primitive gives VM-level isolation for running user- or AI-generated code with near-instant launch and resume, state preservation up to 8 hours, and no infrastructure management. It targets interactive coding environments, data analytics, vulnerability scanners, and multi-tenant AI agents.
items.importance: Serverless isolation just got serious enough for untrusted and AI-generated workloads. This is the cloud-native answer to the same isolation problem Firecracker microVMs solve in homelab and agent sandboxes.
items.noteworthy: If you run AI agents or user code in AWS, evaluate Lambda MicroVMs against ECS/Fargate for isolation needs. Available in US East (N. Virginia/Ohio), US West (Oregon), Tokyo, and Ireland.

items.title: Market Snapshot: BTC 4,009 | ETH ,729 | SOL 1.92
items.desc: Bitcoin flat (+0.06%), Ethereum +0.13%, Solana +2.17%. US-Iran 60-day deal roadmap weighs on oil and supports BTC.
items.summary: As of June 22/23, CoinGecko/Cointelegraph data shows Bitcoin at 4,009 (+0.06%), Ethereum at ,729 (+0.13%), and Solana at 1.92 (+2.17%). The US and Iran agreed on a 60-day roadmap toward a final deal after talks in Switzerland, with Iran receiving oil-export waivers and some frozen-asset releases. Oil dropped to 16-week lows, and BTC steadied near 4K after briefly touching 5,555.
items.importance: 4K remains the consolidation pivot. Geopolitical de-escalation is giving markets room to breathe, but a confirmed break above 6K is still needed to restart bullish momentum.
items.noteworthy: Watch 2K support and 6K resistance. Solana is outperforming ETH and BTC on the day. The next macro catalyst is the 60-day Iran deal deadline and any Fed commentary.

items.title: Ethlabs: Ethereum Veterans Launch Independent R&D Nonprofit
items.desc: Five former Ethereum Foundation researchers, backed by Bitmine, Sharplink, and Joe Lubin, launch Ethlabs to ready Ethereum for institutional adoption.
items.summary: Ethlabs launched June 22, 2026 as an independent nonprofit led by five former senior Ethereum Foundation researchers: Ansgar Dietrichs, Barnabé Monnot, Caspar Schwarz-Schilling, Josh Rudolf, and Julian Ma. Funding comes from Bitmine Immersion Technologies, Sharplink, Consensys founder Joe Lubin, Anchorage, Octant, and SNZ. Ethlabs will focus on faster settlement, native issuance, cross-chain infrastructure, and ETH monetary properties.
items.importance: This is the latest sign of Ethereum's institutional pivot and the externalization of EF research into independent nodes. It also reflects corporate ETH treasuries putting capital behind protocol development.
items.noteworthy: Watch Ethlabs roadmap for mainnet scaling and institutional tooling. If you hold ETH or build on Ethereum, this is a structural evolution in how protocol R&D is funded.

items.title: AutoJack: One Malicious Web Page Can RCE the Host Running Your AI Agent
items.desc: Microsoft's AutoJack turns an AI browsing agent into a host RCE vector by reaching privileged local MCP services from a webpage.
items.summary: Microsoft researchers disclosed AutoJack, an exploit chain where a malicious webpage loaded by an AI browsing agent can pivot to local MCP services and execute code on the host. The attack works through AutoGen Studio's MCP WebSocket in pre-release builds, requiring no credentials or user interaction beyond getting the agent to open the page. It's a concrete demonstration that web-connected AI agents inherit browser-level risks and add host-level consequences.
items.importance: This matters because AI agents are becoming the default interface between users and local tools. AutoJack proves that a prompt injection or malicious link isn't just a chatbot jailbreak — it can become code execution on your machine. Your Hermes agent setup and any MCP-connected tools should be evaluated with this threat model.
items.noteworthy: Segment AI agents from privileged local services. Disable unnecessary MCP servers. Run agents in restricted environments, not your primary workstation. Treat agent-summoned URLs as untrusted content.

items.title: FortiBleed Campaign Compromises 86,644 FortiGate Devices in 194 Countries
items.desc: CISA warns Fortinet customers as Russian-speaking actors mass-harvest FortiGate and VPN credentials. 86,644 devices confirmed hit.
items.summary: CISA issued guidance after a campaign dubbed FortiBleed exposed credentials for 86,644 FortiGate and Fortinet VPN devices across 194 countries. SOCRadar data shows generic admin accounts (35%) and built-in Fortinet system accounts (28.3%) make up most compromised credentials. The attackers are reportedly Russian-speaking and the breach appears to stem from mass credential abuse rather than a single software vulnerability.
items.importance: FortiGate appliances sit on the network perimeter of thousands of organizations. Valid admin credentials give attackers VPN access, lateral movement, and long-term persistence. Even if the device is patched, stale or reused credentials can undo the entire security model.
items.noteworthy: Rotate all FortiGate/FortiVPN admin and service account credentials immediately. Audit user accounts against SOCRadar's leak data. Enforce MFA on VPN and admin access. Disable default system accounts where possible.

items.title: usbliter8: Unpatchable Apple A12/A13 SecureROM Exploit Breaks Boot Chain
items.desc: Paradigm Shift published a working exploit for Apple's A12 and A13 SecureROM. Burned into silicon — no patch possible.
items.summary: Paradigm Shift released usbliter8, an exploit that achieves arbitrary code execution inside the SecureROM of Apple's A12 and A13 chips. The vulnerable code is burned into read-only memory at manufacture, making it unpatchable. The attack requires physical access, DFU mode, and a USB connection to an RP2350-based microcontroller, finishing in under two seconds before Apple's signature checks complete. Affected devices include iPhone XS through 11 series.
items.importance: BootROM exploits are permanent. They jailbreak the root of trust and enable unpatchable forensics, jailbreaks, and potentially malicious device imaging. For Apple-heavy environments, this changes the physical-security risk model — any A12/A13 device is now forever bypassable with hardware access.
items.noteworthy: Treat A12/A13 devices as physically insecure. Use strong device encryption with a complex passphrase. Enforce remote wipe and device tracking. Limit sensitive data on iPhone XS/11-series hardware where physical compromise is a concern.

items.title: Apertus: Fully Open Foundation Model for Sovereign AI
items.desc: Swiss AI Initiative releases Apertus — open weights, open data, open training code, EU AI Act compliant.
items.summary: The Swiss AI Initiative launched Apertus, a fully open foundation model developed by EPFL, ETH Zurich, and CSCS. It releases training data, code, weights, methods, and alignment principles. Models ship at 8B and 70B scales, are competitive with top open models, and are multilingual from day one across 1000+ languages. Apertus is built to comply with the EU AI Act, including opt-out respect, PII removal, and memorization prevention.
items.importance: Sovereign AI is becoming a geopolitical necessity. Apertus is the most transparent major open model effort to date and directly counters proprietary centralization. For EU users and privacy-conscious deployments, it's a credible alternative to US and Chinese models.
items.noteworthy: Watch for Apertus quantized releases on HuggingFace and Ollama. Evaluate it for sensitive workloads where data sovereignty matters. The open training pipeline is as significant as the model weights.

items.title: Sakana Fugu: Multi-Agent System Delivered as a Single Model API
items.desc: Sakana AI's Fugu dynamically orchestrates multiple frontier models through one API, claiming top-tier performance without vendor lock-in.
items.summary: Sakana AI announced Fugu, a multi-agent system packaged as a single model API. Fugu learns to dynamically assemble agents from a pool of diverse models and coordinate them through emergent collaboration patterns rather than prescribed roles. Sakana claims frontier-level performance without dependence on a single vendor. It's currently unavailable in the EU/EEA while GDPR compliance work continues.
items.importance: The routing/orchestration layer may become more valuable than any single model. Fugu is betting that a coordinator model can beat monolithic giants by combining specialized models. If true, it reduces lock-in and changes how you provision model access.
items.noteworthy: Compare Fugu against your current Ollama Cloud setup for complex multi-step tasks. Monitor pricing vs single-model APIs. EU availability is pending — don't plan regulated workloads around it yet.

items.title: Anthropic Now Requires Identity Verification for Claude
items.desc: Anthropic is rolling out government ID verification for Claude, handled by Persona, to access certain capabilities.
items.summary: Anthropic is rolling out identity verification for Claude users, requiring a government-issued photo ID and optional live selfie via partner Persona. The company says verification is needed to prevent abuse, enforce usage policies, and comply with legal obligations. Users will see prompts when accessing specific capabilities or during platform integrity checks.
items.importance: Identity verification in frontier AI is becoming standard. For privacy-conscious users and pseudonymous work, this is a regression. It also creates a single point of failure: a verification partner breach could expose ID + selfie data.
items.noteworthy: Evaluate whether Claude remains viable for anonymous or pseudonymous workflows. Consider local/open alternatives for sensitive tasks. Monitor Persona's privacy policy and data retention terms.

items.title: Google Hits 50% IPv6 Adoption
items.desc: Google's public stats show IPv6 traffic crossing the 50% mark globally, a milestone after decades of migration.
items.summary: APNIC reported that Google has reached 50% IPv6 adoption across its services, based on Google's public IPv6 statistics. The milestone reflects gradual ISP, mobile carrier, and enterprise upgrades. IPv6 is now the majority protocol for Google-facing traffic, though regional variation remains significant — some networks are near 100% while others still rely heavily on IPv4.
items.importance: The IPv4 exhaustion era is effectively ending for major services. Network engineers and homelab operators should ensure IPv6 is enabled and understood in their stacks. Dual-stack configs and IPv6 firewall rules are now baseline skills, not optional extras.
items.noteworthy: Verify your Proxmox, router, and Tailscale setups support IPv6. Review firewall rules for IPv6 — default-deny matters even more when every device gets a public address. Test DNS AAAA records if you run services.

items.title: MiniPCs.zip Maps the Pareto Frontier of Mini PCs
items.desc: Show HN project scans thousands of Mini PCs from Amazon and eBay twice daily and plots price vs performance.
items.summary: MiniPCs.zip is a new Show HN project that performs twice-daily scans of thousands of Mini PCs from Amazon and eBay and plots them on interactive charts. Users can compare price against CPU performance, graphics capability, memory, storage, and other dimensions. It's designed to find the Pareto-optimal Mini PC for a given workload and budget.
items.importance: Mini PCs are the default homelab edge node for Proxmox, Ollama, or networking experiments. A living price/performance index removes guesswork from buying. Useful for your next low-power node or thin client conversion.
items.noteworthy: Use MiniPCs.zip before your next homelab purchase. Compare recent N100/N305/AMD 7840U boxes. Watch for refurbished enterprise thin clients if you want cheap low-power Proxmox nodes.

items.title: Running MicroVMs in Proxmox VE the Easy Way
items.desc: New pve-microvm package brings Firecracker-style QEMU microVMs into Proxmox VE with web UI integration.
items.summary: A project called pve-microvm adds QEMU's microvm machine type as a first-class guest in Proxmox VE. It ships a custom kernel and patches Proxmox's Perl internals to integrate with the web UI. The package supports 21 guest OS types, from Debian to NetBSD to Plan 9, offering faster boot and lower overhead than full VMs while maintaining stronger isolation than LXC containers.
items.importance: MicroVMs fill the gap between containers and full VMs — fast boot, small footprint, hardware isolation. For homelab agent sandboxes, untrusted workloads, or ephemeral compute, this is a big upgrade over LXC for security without the bloat of full VMs.
items.noteworthy: Evaluate pve-microvm for your Proxmox cluster if you run untrusted code, AI agent sandboxes, or ephemeral services. It supports 21 OS types and integrates into the Proxmox web UI.

items.title: GLM-5.2 Hallucinates Far Less Than GPT-5.5 and DeepSeek V4 Pro
items.desc: Analysis argues open-weight GLM-5.2 shows smaller models trained on factual data can beat giant models on hallucination and honesty.
items.summary: A recent analysis claims DeepSeek V4 Pro has a 94% hallucination rate on the AA-Omniscience benchmark — it almost never admits not knowing. GPT-5.5 is described as hallucinating roughly 3x more than MIT-licensed GLM-5.2. The argument is that scaling parameters without improving data quality produces confidently wrong answers, while smaller, factually trained models can be more honest.
items.importance: Hallucination rate matters as much as benchmark scores for production use. If this analysis holds, GLM-5.2 may be more trustworthy for factual tasks than larger proprietary models. It's another data point in the shift away from pure parameter scaling.
items.noteworthy: Test GLM-5.2 against your current models on factual QA tasks where admitting ignorance is acceptable. Monitor independent hallucination benchmarks rather than headline leaderboard scores.

items.title: Two Qwen3 Models on One DGX Spark via vLLM
items.desc: Blog details using vLLM's PagedAttention and gpu_memory_utilization to run two Qwen3 models side-by-side on a single DGX Spark.
items.summary: A developer documented running two Qwen3 models simultaneously on one DGX Spark by switching from Ollama to vLLM. PagedAttention and explicit gpu_memory_utilization settings allow coresident heavy and fast models, which Ollama's llama.cpp backend doesn't support cleanly. The post covers residency math, memory allocation, and verification steps for a multi-model local inference fleet.
items.importance: This is directly relevant to your setup. If you want multiple models on one GPU, Ollama's single-model-at-a-time model is limiting. vLLM unlocks concurrent model serving and better KV cache management.
items.noteworthy: Experiment with vLLM on your DGX Spark or GPU node if you serve multiple models. Compare throughput and memory efficiency against Ollama. Be ready for more configuration complexity.

items.title: Market Snapshot: BTC $64,186 | ETH $1,737 | SOL $73.91
items.desc: Bitcoin flat (+0.08%), Ethereum +0.28%, Solana +1.22%. Market treading water near resistance.
items.summary: CoinGecko data shows Bitcoin at $64,186 with a 24-hour change of +0.08% and market cap of $1.286T. Ethereum trades at $1,736.80 (+0.28%, $209.3B cap). Solana is at $73.91 (+1.22%, $42.8B cap). The market is consolidating near the $64K-$66K range with no strong directional breakout yet.
items.importance: $64K remains the key level. Failure to hold risks a retest of lower support; a clean break above $66K could restart momentum. For now, risk-off consolidation dominates crypto.
items.noteworthy: Watch $66K resistance and $62K support. Solana outperforming suggests alt rotation. No major macro catalyst visible until next week.
